edges.description: Pathogenic variants reduce or alter cohesin structural/regulatory functions required for normal chromatin binding and organization.
edges.target: Impaired cohesin loading and chromatin organization

edges.description: Altered cohesin loading and regulatory-region binding changes expression of developmental genes in CdLS cells and models.
edges.source: Impaired cohesin loading and chromatin organization

edges.description: Dysregulated developmental gene expression perturbs facial, skeletal, and limb morphogenesis.
intermediate_mechanisms: Altered expression of developmental regulator genes

edges.description: Widespread transcriptional perturbation disrupts growth and neural developmental programs.
intermediate_mechanisms: Cumulative subtle changes in growth and neurodevelopmental gene expression programs

edges.description: Multisystem developmental gene dysregulation contributes to GI, cardiac, renal, and other visceral manifestations.
Synophrys is part of the recognizable CdLS facial gestalt.
Limb morphogenesis disruption produces micromelia and related hand-foot abnormalities.
Limb morphogenesis disruption can produce reductional limb defects including oligodactyly.

edges.target: Atresia of the external auditory canal

edges.description: Growth-program disruption causes antenatal growth restriction.
Growth-program disruption causes postnatal short stature.
Neurodevelopmental transcriptional disruption contributes to intellectual disability.
Multisystem developmental disruption contributes to feeding difficulty.
GI developmental and motility disturbance contributes to reflux.
Developmental disruption can produce congenital heart defects including VSD.
intermediate_mechanisms: Altered cardiac developmental gene expression programs

edges.target: Impaired cohesin loading and chromatin organization
Impaired cohesin loading and chromatin organization
Impaired cohesin loading and chromatin organization
Impaired cohesin loading and chromatin organization

nodes.description: CdLS is caused by pathogenic variants in genes encoding cohesin structural proteins or regulators, including NIPBL, SMC1A, SMC3, RAD21, HDAC8, and BRD4. NIPBL encodes the delangin/cohesin-loading factor, and many cases are driven by reduced dosage or impaired function of this pathway.

evidence.reference_title: Cornelia de Lange syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: NIPBL | NIPBL cohesin loading factor | hgnc:28862 | Disease-causing germline mutation(s) in

evidence.reference_title: Diagnosis and management of Cornelia de Lange syndrome: first international consensus statement.
evidence.snippet: It is caused by variants in any one of seven genes, all of which have a structural or regulatory function in the cohesin complex.

evidence.reference_title: Cornelia de Lange syndrome is caused by mutations in NIPBL, the human homolog of Drosophila melanogaster Nipped-B.
evidence.snippet: We identified mutations in one gene in this region, which we named NIPBL, in four sporadic and two familial cases of CdLS.

nodes.description: Compulsive behaviors are frequent behavioral manifestations in CdLS.

evidence.reference_title: Cornelia de Lange syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0000722 | Compulsive behaviors | Frequent (79-30%)

nodes.description: CdLS shows a recognizable craniofacial gestalt and upper-limb/hand-foot malformations, including synophrys, thick eyebrows, long eyelashes, micrognathia, small hands, micromelia, and oligodactyly.

evidence.reference_title: Cornelia de Lange syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: facial dysmorphism, hypertrichosis, mild to profound intellectual disability, intrauterine growth restriction (IUGR) and/or postnatal growth restriction, feeding difficulties, abnormalities of the hands and feet

evidence.reference_title: NIPBL, encoding a homolog of fungal Scc2-type sister chromatid cohesion proteins and fly Nipped-B, is mutated in Cornelia de Lange syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We propose that perturbed delangin function may inappropriately activate DLX genes, thereby contributing to the proximodistal limb patterning defects in CdLS.

evidence.reference_title: A series of 38 novel germline and somatic mutations of NIPBL in Cornelia de Lange syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Cornelia de Lange syndrome is a multisystemic developmental disorder mainly related to de novo heterozygous NIPBL mutation.

evidence.reference_title: A series of 38 novel germline and somatic mutations of NIPBL in Cornelia de Lange syndrome.
evidence.snippet: All mutations shown to have arisen de novo when parent blood samples were available.

evidence.snippet: HP:0005280 | Depressed nasal bridge | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: Human CdLS cell lines and Nipbl-deficient model systems show widespread modest transcriptional changes whose aggregate effects perturb embryonic development. Cohesin can affect RNA polymerase II initiation and elongation, providing a mechanistic link from cohesin gene variants to multisystem developmental phenotypes.

biological_processes: regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

evidence.reference_title: Transcriptional dysregulation in NIPBL and cohesin mutant human cells.
evidence.snippet: Genome-wide assessment of transcription in 16 mutant cell lines from severely affected CdLS probands has identified a unique profile of dysregulated gene expression that was validated in an additional 101 samples and correlates with phenotypic severity.

evidence.reference_title: Mutant cohesin affects RNA polymerase II regulation in Cornelia de Lange syndrome.
evidence.snippet: altered transcriptional initiation and elongation caused transcription dysregulation in genes whose SMC1A binding was increased.

evidence.reference_title: Multiple organ system defects and transcriptional dysregulation in the Nipbl(+/-) mouse, a model of Cornelia de Lange Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Mice heterozygous for a gene-trap mutation in Nipbl were produced and exhibited defects characteristic of CdLS

nodes.description: Downturned mouth corners are a very frequent facial gestalt feature.

evidence.reference_title: Cornelia de Lange syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: HP:0002714 | Downturned corners of mouth | Very frequent (99-80%)

nodes.description: CdLS commonly includes feeding difficulties and gastroesophageal reflux, and can include renal, genital, cardiac, diaphragmatic, intestinal, and other visceral malformations.

evidence.reference_title: Cornelia de Lange syndrome (Orphanet structured-database record)
evidence.snippet: Variable visceral malformations may be present.

evidence.reference_title: Cornelia de Lange syndrome is caused by mutations in NIPBL, the human homolog of Drosophila melanogaster Nipped-B.
evidence.snippet: gastroesophageal dysfunction; cardiac, ophthalmologic and genitourinary anomalies; hirsutism; and characteristic facial features.

nodes.description: Disrupted cohesin-regulated developmental transcription contributes to antenatal and postnatal growth restriction, microcephaly, intellectual disability, speech impairment, tone abnormalities, autism, anxiety, and other neurobehavioral manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Diagnosis and management of Cornelia de Lange syndrome: first international consensus statement.
evidence.snippet: Cornelia de Lange syndrome (CdLS) is an archetypical genetic syndrome that is characterized by intellectual disability, well-defined facial features, upper limb anomalies and atypical growth, among numerous other signs and symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Multiple organ system defects and transcriptional dysregulation in the Nipbl(+/-) mouse, a model of Cornelia de Lange Syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We propose that the aggregate effects of many small transcriptional changes are the cause of developmental abnormalities of CdLS

nodes.description: NIPBL facilitates cohesin loading and cohesin binding to regulatory regions. CdLS-associated NIPBL or cohesin subunit dysfunction perturbs cohesin binding, chromatin regulatory architecture, and enhancer/promoter control.
nodes.id: Impaired cohesin loading and chromatin organization

evidence.reference_title: NIPBL, encoding a homolog of fungal Scc2-type sister chromatid cohesion proteins and fly Nipped-B, is mutated in Cornelia de Lange syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Vertebrate delangins have substantial homology to orthologs in flies, worms, plants and fungi, including Scc2-type sister chromatid cohesion proteins, and D. melanogaster Nipped-B.

evidence.reference_title: Transcriptional dysregulation in NIPBL and cohesin mutant human cells.
evidence.snippet: Cohesin binding analysis demonstrates a preference for intergenic regions suggesting a cis-regulatory function mimicking that of a boundary/insulator interacting protein.

evidence.reference_title: Cornelia de Lange syndrome is caused by mutations in NIPBL, the human homolog of Drosophila melanogaster Nipped-B.
evidence.snippet: Cornelia de Lange syndrome (CdLS; OMIM 122470) is a dominantly inherited multisystem developmental disorder

evidence.reference_title: Mutant cohesin affects RNA polymerase II regulation in Cornelia de Lange syndrome.
evidence.snippet: About 70% of CdLS patients have heterozygous mutations in NIPBL, the majority being truncating (haploinsufficient), and these types of mutations are often associated with severe phenotypes

evidence.reference_title: NIPBL / Cornelia de Lange syndrome (Definitive)
evidence.snippet: NIPBL | HGNC:28862 | Cornelia de Lange syndrome | MONDO:0016033 | AD | Definitive

evidence.reference_title: High rate of mosaicism in individuals with Cornelia de Lange syndrome.
evidence.snippet: In 10 of the 13 tested individuals without detectable mutation in lymphocytes a NIPBL mutation could be detected in buccal cells.

evidence.reference_title: High rate of mosaicism in individuals with Cornelia de Lange syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Somatic mosaicism for an NIPBL mutation is frequent (10/44; 23%) clinically in reliably diagnosed CdLS individuals.
